title: caulanthus procerus
intro: Caulanthus procerus, also known as prostrate jewelflower, is a species of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae, characterized by its prostrate or ascending growth habit and small, white or yellowish flowers. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to stimulate digestion and relieve indigestion and nausea. Caulanthus procerus is used to treat a variety of health issues, including poor digestion, bloating, and stomach cramps, and its extracts have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. In herbal preparations, Caulanthus procerus is often used as an ingredient in teas, tinctures, and infusions, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat digestive disorders and promote overall well-being.
